title: Foundry Partners
compiled_truth:
Foundry Partners is an early-stage generalist venture fund that has quietly built one of the stronger infrastructure portfolios in its peer group. The firm operates out of a modest office in downtown Chicago, managing roughly $380M across three funds. Their thesis is broad — they'll look at anything from developer tools to biotech platforms — but the partners have a well-known soft spot for picks-and-shovels plays in data infrastructure. The firm's reputation leans on consistency rather than flashy mega-rounds; they prefer leading seed and Series A checks in the $2–6M range and following on selectively.

The fund is led by [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei), who joined as a founding partner in 2019 after a decade at a larger multi-stage firm. Dev's background is in distributed systems, and he's the one who typically sources infrastructure deals. His counterpart [Eli Nakamura](people/eli-nakamura) handles the firm's broader generalist mandate, covering everything from health tech to consumer SaaS. Together they run a lean operation — two partners, one principal, a couple of associates, and a small back-office team.

Foundry first showed up on Anchor Codec's radar in mid-2026. Dev had been tracking the deterministic data infrastructure space for a while and reached out after seeing Anchor's early benchmarks circulate in a private Slack group. The initial conversations were exploratory but moved fast. Foundry ended up participating in Anchor's seed round and has maintained a close working relationship since then.

What makes Foundry unusual among generalist funds is how hands-on they get with portfolio companies on the technical side. Dev sits in on architecture reviews, makes introductions to infrastructure teams at larger enterprises, and occassionally even reviews API documentation. This level of involvement has earned them a strong reputation among technical founders, though some portfolio CEOs have noted it can feel a bit intense at times.

The firm's recent moves suggest they're doubling down on infrastructure. Their latest fund, Fund III, closed in early 2027 at $150M — oversubscribed — with an explicit allocation for what they call "foundational data primitives." Anchor Codec is one of the reference investments they cite when pitching LPs on this thesis. Foundry Partners remains a relatively under-the-radar fund compared to coastal peers, but their deal flow has improved markedly as the infrastucture investment cycle heats up.
timeline:
- **2026-05-14** | [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei) first reaches out to Anchor Codec team after seeing early benchmark data shared in a private infrastructure-focused community.
- **2026-06-22** | Foundry Partners conducts initial diligence call with Anchor leadership; conversation focused on single-pass primitives and market positioning.
- **2026-08-09** | Foundry participates in Anchor Codec's seed round, committing a $3.2M check as co-lead.
- **2026-11-03** | [Eli Nakamura](people/eli-nakamura) joins Anchor's monthly advisory call for the first time, offering go-to-market perspective from Foundry's health tech portfolio.
- **2027-01-18** | Foundry Partners closes Fund III at $150M, oversubscribed by ~20%. Infrastructure thesis prominently features Anchor Codec as a reference investment.
- **2027-04-11** | Dev Osei moderates a panel at a Chicago infrastructure meetup; Anchor's CTO participates remotely.
- **2027-07-26** | Internal Foundry memo circulates recommending follow-on investment in Anchor Codec's Series A, pending updated metrics review.
- **2027-09-15** | Foundry follows on in Anchor's Series A with an additional allocation. Dev takes a board observer seat.
- **2028-02-04** | [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei) publishes a blog post titled "Why Deterministic Infrastructure Matters" — widely shared among enterprise buyers.
- **2028-05-20** | Foundry Partners hosts an LP day in Chicago; Anchor Codec demoed as a portfolio highlight alongside two other infrastructure companies.

_facts.name: Foundry Partners
_facts.description: Early-stage generalist fund with strong infrastructure track record
